skill.name: neuropixels-analysis
skill.description: Analyze Neuropixels extracellular recordings with SpikeInterface workflows for loading, preprocessing, motion correction, spike sorting, quality metrics, curation, visualization, and export.
skill.summary: End-to-end Neuropixels analysis workflows for SpikeGLX, Open Ephys, NWB, Kilosort, quality metrics, and unit curation.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-severity alerts, but review found they are mostly false positives from markdown code fences, neuroscience terms, plotting labels, and API examples. The remaining risks are expected for a scientific analysis skill: local scripts write output files, run SpikeInterface sorters, and document optional AI API use with caller-provided clients. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, persistence, or malicious network behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static command execution findings are mostly false positives
low_findings.description: The scanner reported hundreds of Ruby backtick executions, but reviewed examples are markdown code fences and Python workflow snippets. The skill does include expected SpikeInterface sorter calls, which may invoke installed scientific tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed flagged markdown and script locations show normal Python examples and controlled sorter names. No shell-string construction or user-controlled command injection was found.

low_findings.title: Expected local filesystem writes in analysis scripts
low_findings.description: The scripts create output folders and write plots, metrics, labels, reports, and Phy exports. This is normal for data analysis, but users should choose output paths carefully to avoid overwriting work.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct file writes are present and scoped to configured output directories. The behavior is functional output generation, not stealthy modification or persistence.

low_findings.title: Optional AI API examples require user-provided clients
low_findings.description: The documentation shows Anthropic and OpenAI client examples for visual unit curation. No hardcoded secret or automatic data upload was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API usage is documented as optional and requires an explicit client object. The static secret-key hits are placeholders, not embedded credentials.

low_findings.title: Weak crypto and C2 findings are domain false positives
low_findings.description: Reviewed flagged terms refer to neuroscience concepts, plot annotations, curation labels, quality metrics, and sorter names. No cryptographic code, command-and-control logic, or suspicious prompt injection text was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain spike sorting, correlogram, histogram, and unit-quality terminology. I did not find evidence of malware intent or cryptographic misuse.

content.value_statement: Neuropixels analysis often requires many fragile preprocessing, sorting, and curation steps. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured workflows for SpikeInterface-based analysis and review.
actual_capabilities: Guide loading SpikeGLX, Open Ephys, and NWB recordings through SpikeInterface readers.
Describe preprocessing steps including filtering, phase shift correction, bad channel detection, and common median reference.
Support motion and drift review with SpikeInterface motion correction workflows.
Run or adapt scripts for Kilosort4, SpykingCircus2, MountainSort5, and related sorting workflows.
Compute quality metrics and apply Allen, IBL, or strict unit curation criteria.
Generate plots, reports, Phy exports, NWB exports, and AI-assisted visual curation prompts.
limitations: Requires installed scientific Python packages and compatible SpikeInterface sorter dependencies.
Kilosort workflows may require a GPU and local sorter installation.
AI visual curation is advisory and should not replace expert review for critical experiments.
Users must provide valid local data paths and manage large output files.

use_cases.title: Process a New Recording
use_cases.description: Load raw Neuropixels data, preprocess channels, check drift, and prepare data for sorting.

use_cases.title: Standardize Spike Sorting
use_cases.description: Run a repeatable SpikeInterface pipeline with documented sorter choices and curation thresholds.

use_cases.title: Review Borderline Units
use_cases.description: Combine quality metrics, waveform plots, correlograms, and AI-assisted visual review for uncertain units.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect My Recording
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check of raw Neuropixels data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me inspect a Neuropixels recording at [DATA_PATH]. Identify the format, list basic metadata, and recommend preprocessing steps.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Preprocessing Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a preprocessing plan for a Neuropixels [PROBE_VERSION] recording using [FORMAT]. Include filtering, phase shift, bad channels, reference, and drift checks.

prompt_templates.title: Run Sorting and Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a SpikeInterface workflow that preprocesses [DATA_PATH], runs [SORTER], computes quality metrics, and saves outputs under [OUTPUT_DIR].

prompt_templates.title: Curate Borderline Units
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these unit metrics and plots from [OUTPUT_DIR]. Classify good, multi-unit, noise, and uncertain units using Allen or IBL criteria.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a first-pass review of a SpikeGLX Neuropixels folder.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step inspection plan covering stream detection, channel count, duration, sampling rate, bad channels, and example plots.

output_examples.input: A user wants to compare Kilosort4 results against quality thresholds.
output_examples.output: A concise analysis report describing sorter settings, metric summaries, good unit counts, rejected units, and recommended manual review targets.

output_examples.input: A user provides waveform and autocorrelogram plots for uncertain units.
output_examples.output: A curation table in prose that labels each unit, explains the evidence, and flags units requiring human confirmation.

best_practices: Run short pilot slices before processing full recordings to verify paths, formats, and sorter settings.
Record all preprocessing, sorter, and curation parameters with each analysis output.
Use AI visual review only after quantitative metrics and plots are generated.
anti_patterns: Do not run GPU sorters on full datasets before checking drift and bad channels.
Do not treat automated unit labels as final without reviewing important experiments.
Do not reuse thresholds across projects without checking probe type, brain region, and recording quality.

faq.question: What data formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: It documents workflows for SpikeGLX, Open Ephys, and NWB recordings through SpikeInterface readers.

faq.question: Can it run Kilosort4?
faq.answer: Yes, the scripts and guidance include Kilosort4 through SpikeInterface when dependencies and hardware are installed.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: Core local analysis does not require internet access. Optional AI visual curation examples may use external API clients.

faq.question: Does it replace manual spike sorting review?
faq.answer: No. It supports automated review, but important experiments still need expert inspection and documented decisions.

faq.question: Can I export results to Phy?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes Phy export guidance for manual curation and downstream review.

faq.question: Why was the security risk not high?
faq.answer: Human review found the high static alerts were false positives from markdown, scientific terms, and normal analysis scripts.
